oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Parasites for new graduates: Shifting mindsets toward sustainable parasite control in beef cattle
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Navarre, Christine B.
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: anthelmintic resistance
gastrointestinal nematodes
refugia
beef cattle
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Anthelmintic resistance among gastrointestinal nematodes of beef cattle is now widespread in the United States and rep­resents a major threat to long-term herd health, productivity and veterinary sustainability. For decades, parasite manage­ment relied on highly effective, convenient dewormers applied using standardized programs. Declining efficacy of existing compounds, combined with the absence of new drug classes in the development pipeline, necessitates a shift from routine deworming to integrated parasite control. This paper outlines best management practices for parasite control in beef cattle, emphasizing diagnostics, refugia-based strategies, appropriate use of combination anthelmintics, and selection for parasite-resistant and parasite-resilient cattle. Emphasis is placed on tailoring recommendations to individual ranches based on cli­mate, management, genetics and stressors. Adoption of these strategies can slow progression of resistance, maintain animal health and performance, and support long-term sustainability of beef production systems.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 21-22
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
